card.intuition: dp[a] = minimum coins to make amount a. For each coin c, dp[a] = min(dp[a], dp[a-c] + 1). Because coins can be reused, iterate amount from 0 to target — same as unbounded knapsack. Initialise dp[0]=0, all others ∞.
card.analogy: Making change at a till: for each target amount, consider using every coin denomination and keep track of the minimum coins needed. Build up from 1 cent to the target.
card.time_complexity: O(amount · n) where n = number of coin denominations
when_to_use: Number of ways to make change (change count DP variant)
Any unbounded item selection problem with a sum target
gotchas: Initialise dp[0]=0 and dp[1..amount]=∞ — not 0, to distinguish 'reachable with 0 coins' from 'unreachable'
If dp[amount] is still ∞ after filling, return -1 (no solution)
Greedy (always pick the largest coin) does NOT work for all coin sets — use DP

exercise.prompt: Implement coin change: minimum number of coins to make the target amount.
hints: If coin c fits (c <= a), try dp[a-c] + 1 — one more coin than what was needed for amount a-c
exercise.test_code:
assert coin_change([1,5,10,25], 36) == 3   # 25+10+1
assert coin_change([1,5,11], 15) == 3       # 11+1+1+1+1? No: 5+5+5=3 ✓
assert coin_change([2], 3) == -1            # impossible
assert coin_change([1], 0) == 0
assert coin_change([1,2,5], 11) == 3        # 5+5+1
print('PASS')
